EDA 72: Predisposition Profiles Across Chronic-Absence Groups

Extending the EDA-71 G2 Outcome Family

Author

Analysis Team

Published

July 19, 2026

Mission

This EDA extends the EDA-71 G2 family by relating its outcome groups and exact chronic-day distribution to the non-student predisposing variables documented in Section 3 of the Data Primer. It first profiles respondents with No Days, then makes the proposed day-scale coarsening explicit, and finally compares No Days with Chronic Days while retaining the positive chronic-day values.

Research Questions:

  1. How are predisposing variables distributed among respondents with No Days?
  2. How do exact chronic days map to weekly and hybrid coarsened scales?
  3. How does predisposing composition differ between No Days and Chronic Days?
  4. How does chronic-day intensity vary across predisposing-variable levels?
  5. Which predictor levels have visibly different exact chronic-day distributions?

Definition of Terms

No Days: days_absent_total == 0 (n = 45,006).

Chronic Days: days_absent_chronic > 0 (n = 3,626). This group may also report non-chronic absence days.

Non-Chronic Days: days_absent_chronic == 0 & days_absent_total > 0 (n = 15,211).

Work-week coarsening: Five reported workdays are grouped into one display week. This is a scale-design choice, not a claim about elapsed time or work schedule.

Environment

Packages used in the current report 
library(magrittr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)
library(purrr)
library(scales)
library(fs)
requireNamespace("arrow", quietly = TRUE)
Project functions and Alberta visual identity colours 
base::source("./scripts/common-functions.R")
base::source("./scripts/graphing/graph-presets.R")
Paths, dimensions, predictor definitions, and group labels 
local_root <- "./analysis/eda-72/"
local_data <- paste0(local_root, "data-local/")
prints_folder <- paste0(local_root, "prints/")
data_private_derived <- "./data-private/derived/eda-72/"
path_parquet <- "./data-private/derived/cchs-2-tables/cchs_analytic.parquet"
weight_col <- "wts_m_pooled"
a4_landscape_width <- 11.69
a4_landscape_height <- 8.27

if (!fs::dir_exists(local_data)) { fs::dir_create(local_data) }
if (!fs::dir_exists(prints_folder)) { fs::dir_create(prints_folder) }
if (!fs::dir_exists(data_private_derived)) {
  fs::dir_create(data_private_derived, recurse = TRUE)
}

predisposition_specs <- tibble::tribble(
  ~variable, ~variable_label,
  "age_group_3", "Age group",
  "dhhgage", "Age (5-year group)",
  "sex", "Sex",
  "marital_status", "Marital status",
  "household_size", "Household size",
  "dhhgle5", "Children age 0-5",
  "dhhg611", "Children age 6-11",
  "education", "Education",
  "immigration_status", "Immigration status",
  "visible_minority", "Visible minority",
  "living_arrangements", "Living arrangements"
)

outcome_group_levels <- c("No Days", "Chronic Days", "Non-Chronic Days")
comparison_group_levels <- c("No Days", "Chronic Days")
comparison_group_colours <- c(
  "No Days" = abcol[["grey"]],
  "Chronic Days" = abcol[["brown"]],
  "Non-Chronic Days" = abcol[["blue"]]
)

dhhgage_labels <- c(
  "2" = "15-17 yrs", "3" = "18-19 yrs", "4" = "20-24 yrs",
  "5" = "25-29 yrs", "6" = "30-34 yrs", "7" = "35-39 yrs",
  "8" = "40-44 yrs", "9" = "45-49 yrs", "10" = "50-54 yrs",
  "11" = "55-59 yrs", "12" = "60-64 yrs", "13" = "65-69 yrs",
  "14" = "70-74 yrs", "15" = "75-79 yrs"
)
Helpers for predictor reshaping, facet labels, and weighted means 
make_predictor_long <- function(data, specifications) {
  purrr::map2_dfr(
    specifications$variable,
    specifications$variable_label,
    function(variable_name, display_name) {
      source_value <- data[[variable_name]]
      if (identical(variable_name, "dhhgage")) {
        value_levels <- names(dhhgage_labels)
        level_value <- factor(
          dhhgage_labels[as.character(source_value)],
          levels = unname(dhhgage_labels[value_levels])
        )
      } else {
        value_levels <- if (is.factor(source_value)) {
          levels(source_value)
        } else {
          sort(unique(stats::na.omit(source_value))) |> as.character()
        }
        level_value <- factor(as.character(source_value), levels = value_levels)
      }

      tibble::tibble(
        respondent_id = data$.respondent_id,
        outcome_group = data$outcome_group,
        weight_value = data$weight_value,
        days_absent_chronic = data$days_absent_chronic,
        variable = variable_name,
        variable_label = display_name,
        level = level_value
      )
    }
  )
}

add_facet_level <- function(data) {
  ordered_levels <- data %>%
    distinct(variable_label, level) %>%
    mutate(facet_level = paste(variable_label, level, sep = "___")) %>%
    pull(facet_level)

  data %>%
    mutate(
      facet_level = factor(
        paste(variable_label, level, sep = "___"),
        levels = rev(unique(ordered_levels))
      )
    )
}

strip_facet_level <- function(value) sub("^.*___", "", value)

weighted_mean <- function(value, weight) {
  stats::weighted.mean(value, weight, na.rm = TRUE)
}

compose_two_panel_grob <- function(left_plot, right_plot, left_width = 0.22, right_width = 0.78) {
  left_grob <- ggplot2::ggplotGrob(left_plot)
  right_grob <- ggplot2::ggplotGrob(right_plot)

  grid::grid.grabExpr({
    grid::grid.newpage()
    grid::pushViewport(
      grid::viewport(
        layout = grid::grid.layout(
          nrow = 1,
          ncol = 2,
          widths = grid::unit(c(left_width, right_width), "npc")
        )
      )
    )
    grid::pushViewport(grid::viewport(layout.pos.row = 1, layout.pos.col = 1))
    grid::grid.draw(left_grob)
    grid::upViewport()
    grid::pushViewport(grid::viewport(layout.pos.row = 1, layout.pos.col = 2))
    grid::grid.draw(right_grob)
    grid::upViewport(2)
  }, wrap.grobs = TRUE)
}

Data

Load the Ellis respondent-level analytical parquet 
ds0 <- arrow::read_parquet(path_parquet) %>%
  mutate(
    .respondent_id = dplyr::row_number(),
    outcome_group = case_when(
      days_absent_total == 0 ~ "No Days",
      days_absent_chronic > 0 ~ "Chronic Days",
      days_absent_chronic == 0 & days_absent_total > 0 ~ "Non-Chronic Days",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    outcome_group = factor(outcome_group, levels = outcome_group_levels),
    weight_value = .data[[weight_col]],
    days_absent_chronic = as.integer(days_absent_chronic)
  )

missing_predictors <- setdiff(predisposition_specs$variable, names(ds0))
if (length(missing_predictors) > 0) {
  stop("Missing predisposing variables: ", paste(missing_predictors, collapse = ", "))
}

predictor_long <- make_predictor_long(ds0, predisposition_specs)
Verify the three outcome-group counts and chronic-day support 
outcome_group_summary <- ds0 %>%
  filter(!is.na(outcome_group)) %>%
  count(outcome_group, name = "n_raw") %>%
  mutate(expected_n = c(45006L, 3626L, 15211L))

stopifnot(all(outcome_group_summary$n_raw == outcome_group_summary$expected_n))
stopifnot(max(ds0$days_absent_chronic, na.rm = TRUE) == 31L)

n_no_days <- outcome_group_summary %>% filter(outcome_group == "No Days") %>% pull(n_raw)
n_chronic_days <- outcome_group_summary %>% filter(outcome_group == "Chronic Days") %>% pull(n_raw)
n_non_chronic_days <- outcome_group_summary %>% filter(outcome_group == "Non-Chronic Days") %>% pull(n_raw)

group_definition_short <- paste0(
  "No Days: n = ", scales::comma(n_no_days),
  "; Chronic: >=1 chronic day (n = ", scales::comma(n_chronic_days),
  "); Non-Chronic: >0 missed days and 0 chronic days (n = ", scales::comma(n_non_chronic_days), ")."
)

Analysis

Graph Family g0: No Days Predisposition Overview

The No Days group is the largest outcome group. These panels show each predictor’s weighted composition without implying that the categories across different predictors share a common denominator beyond membership in the same No Days cohort.

Prepare weighted predisposing-variable distributions for No Days 
g0_data <- predictor_long %>%
  filter(outcome_group == "No Days", !is.na(level)) %>%
  group_by(variable, variable_label, level) %>%
  summarise(
    n_raw = n(),
    weight_sum = sum(weight_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop_last"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_weighted = weight_sum / sum(weight_sum) * 100,
    label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct_weighted)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  add_facet_level()

g01 - A4 Landscape Design

Graph g01 tests the same content at A4 landscape dimensions. Three facet columns, larger labels, and direct percentages trade browser compactness for print legibility.

Graph Family g1: Chronic-Day Scale Design

The exact support contains integer values from 1 through 31. Graph g1 exposes rather than hides the consequence of each coarsening rule: exact values, five-day work-week bins, and a hybrid that retains one-day granularity through day 5 before coarsening.

Map every exact chronic-day value to each proposed scale 
g1_scale_mapping <- tidyr::crossing(
  days_absent_chronic = 1:31,
  scale = factor(
    c("Exact reported days", "Five-day work weeks", "First week exact; tail coarsened"),
    levels = c("Exact reported days", "First week exact; tail coarsened", "Five-day work weeks")
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    bin_index = case_when(
      scale == "Exact reported days" ~ days_absent_chronic,
      scale == "Five-day work weeks" ~ ceiling(days_absent_chronic / 5),
      days_absent_chronic <= 5 ~ days_absent_chronic,
      TRUE ~ 5 + ceiling((days_absent_chronic - 5) / 5)
    ),
    bin_label = case_when(
      scale == "Exact reported days" ~ as.character(days_absent_chronic),
      scale == "Five-day work weeks" & days_absent_chronic == 31 ~ "7+ weeks",
      scale == "Five-day work weeks" ~ paste0(ceiling(days_absent_chronic / 5), " week", if_else(ceiling(days_absent_chronic / 5) == 1, "", "s")),
      days_absent_chronic <= 5 ~ paste0(days_absent_chronic, " day", if_else(days_absent_chronic == 1, "", "s")),
      days_absent_chronic == 31 ~ "31 days",
      TRUE ~ paste0((ceiling((days_absent_chronic - 5) / 5) - 1) * 5 + 6, "-", ceiling((days_absent_chronic - 5) / 5) * 5 + 5, " days")
    ),
    band = factor(bin_index %% 2)
  )

g1_scale_labels <- g1_scale_mapping %>%
  group_by(scale, bin_index, bin_label) %>%
  summarise(label_x = mean(days_absent_chronic), .groups = "drop")

g11 - Chronic-Day Distribution at Three Scales

Graph g11 adopts the left-hand three-way cohort partition from EDA-71 graph g22. The three stacked panels at right hold the same 3,626 Chronic respondents (defined as respondents with at least one chronic day) at the exact, five-day work-week, and first-week-exact scales. The top panel uses an exact-day colour category; the lower panels use the seven five-day work-week colour bands. Therefore, in the bottom panel days 1 through 5 remain separate bars while sharing the first work-week colour.

Prepare the three-way cohort partition and chronic-day frequencies at three scales 
# Extend the EDA-71 G22 composition: the unchanged three-way respondent partition
# sits at left, while the chronic-reporter distribution is shown at three scales.
g11_partition_summary <- ds0 %>%
  filter(!is.na(outcome_group)) %>%
  count(outcome_group, name = "n_raw") %>%
  mutate(
    pct_raw = n_raw / sum(n_raw) * 100,
    outcome_group = factor(outcome_group, levels = outcome_group_levels)
  )

g11_chronic_exact <- ds0 %>%
  filter(outcome_group == "Chronic Days") %>%
  count(days_absent_chronic, name = "n_raw") %>%
  tidyr::complete(days_absent_chronic = 1:31, fill = list(n_raw = 0L)) %>%
  mutate(
    exact_day = factor(days_absent_chronic, levels = 1:31),
    work_week = pmin(ceiling(days_absent_chronic / 5), 7L),
    work_week_label = factor(
      case_when(
        work_week == 1 ~ "1-5 days (1 week)",
        work_week == 2 ~ "6-10 days (2 weeks)",
        work_week == 3 ~ "11-15 days (3 weeks)",
        work_week == 4 ~ "16-20 days (4 weeks)",
        work_week == 5 ~ "21-25 days (5 weeks)",
        work_week == 6 ~ "26-30 days (6 weeks)",
        TRUE ~ "31 days (7+ weeks)"
      ),
      levels = c(
        "1-5 days (1 week)", "6-10 days (2 weeks)",
        "11-15 days (3 weeks)", "16-20 days (4 weeks)",
        "21-25 days (5 weeks)", "26-30 days (6 weeks)",
        "31 days (7+ weeks)"
      )
    ),
    hybrid_label = factor(
      case_when(
        days_absent_chronic <= 5 ~ paste0(days_absent_chronic, " day", if_else(days_absent_chronic == 1, "", "s")),
        days_absent_chronic <= 10 ~ "6-10 days",
        days_absent_chronic <= 15 ~ "11-15 days",
        days_absent_chronic <= 20 ~ "16-20 days",
        days_absent_chronic <= 25 ~ "21-25 days",
        days_absent_chronic <= 30 ~ "26-30 days",
        TRUE ~ "31 days"
      ),
      levels = c("1 day", "2 days", "3 days", "4 days", "5 days", "6-10 days",
                 "11-15 days", "16-20 days", "21-25 days", "26-30 days", "31 days")
    )
  )

g11_weekly <- g11_chronic_exact %>%
  group_by(work_week, work_week_label) %>%
  summarise(n_raw = sum(n_raw), .groups = "drop")

g11_hybrid <- g11_chronic_exact %>%
  group_by(hybrid_label, work_week_label) %>%
  summarise(n_raw = sum(n_raw), .groups = "drop")

g11_partition_colours <- c(
  "No Days" = "#D9D9D9",
  "Chronic Days" = "#D08C60",
  "Non-Chronic Days" = "#0B4F6C"
)
g11_exact_colours <- stats::setNames(grDevices::hcl.colors(31, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE), as.character(1:31))
g11_week_colours <- stats::setNames(
  grDevices::hcl.colors(7, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE),
  levels(g11_chronic_exact$work_week_label)
)

g12 - Hybrid Missed-Day Distributions by Positive-Day Group

Graph g12 now keeps the left-hand three-way stacked partition and adds a right-hand comparison for the two positive-day groups only: Chronic (>=1 chronic day; n=3,626) and Non-Chronic (>0 missed days and 0 chronic days; n=15,211). Within each group, the first panel preserves days 1 through 5 while the second panel groups the remaining tail into work-week bins 2 through 7+. Panel widths are proportional to their five and six bars, so an individual bar occupies approximately the same width on both sides.

Prepare hybrid missed-day frequencies for Chronic and Non-Chronic Days groups 
# Compare the hybrid missed-day distribution for the two positive-day groups.
g12_partition_summary <- g11_partition_summary

g12_hybrid_distribution <- ds0 %>%
  filter(outcome_group %in% c("Chronic Days", "Non-Chronic Days")) %>%
  transmute(
    outcome_group,
    missed_days = if_else(
      outcome_group == "Chronic Days",
      days_absent_chronic,
      as.integer(days_absent_total)
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    bin_index = case_when(
      missed_days <= 5 ~ missed_days,
      missed_days <= 10 ~ 6L,
      missed_days <= 15 ~ 7L,
      missed_days <= 20 ~ 8L,
      missed_days <= 25 ~ 9L,
      missed_days <= 30 ~ 10L,
      TRUE ~ 11L
    )
  ) %>%
  count(outcome_group, bin_index, name = "n_raw") %>%
  tidyr::complete(
    outcome_group = factor(
      c("Chronic Days", "Non-Chronic Days"),
      levels = c("Chronic Days", "Non-Chronic Days")
    ),
    bin_index = 1:11,
    fill = list(n_raw = 0L)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    scale_panel = factor(
      if_else(bin_index <= 5, "A Week or Less", "More than a Week"),
      levels = c("A Week or Less", "More than a Week")
    ),
    bin_label = factor(
      case_when(
        bin_index <= 5 ~ as.character(bin_index),
        bin_index == 6 ~ "2",
        bin_index == 7 ~ "3",
        bin_index == 8 ~ "4",
        bin_index == 9 ~ "5",
        bin_index == 10 ~ "6",
        TRUE ~ "7+"
      ),
      levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7+")
    ),
    work_week = factor(
      if_else(bin_index <= 5, 1L, bin_index - 4L),
      levels = 1:7
    )
  )

Graph Family g2: Outcome-Group Predisposition Comparisons

This family compares group composition before moving to the numeric chronic-day values. Percentages in g2, g20, g21, g211, and g212 use the pooled survey weight. Graphs g22 through g24 condition on the 3,626 Chronic Days respondents.

Prepare weighted composition contrasts and chronic-day intensity 
g2_composition_all <- predictor_long %>%
  filter(outcome_group %in% outcome_group_levels, !is.na(level)) %>%
  group_by(outcome_group, variable, variable_label, level) %>%
  summarise(
    n_raw = n(),
    weight_sum = sum(weight_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop_last"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_raw = n_raw / sum(n_raw) * 100,
    pct_weighted = weight_sum / sum(weight_sum) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(outcome_group = factor(outcome_group, levels = outcome_group_levels)) %>%
  add_facet_level()

g2_composition <- g2_composition_all %>%
  filter(outcome_group %in% comparison_group_levels) %>%
  mutate(outcome_group = factor(outcome_group, levels = comparison_group_levels))

g2_difference <- g2_composition %>%
  select(variable, variable_label, level, outcome_group, pct_weighted, facet_level) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = outcome_group, values_from = pct_weighted) %>%
  mutate(difference_pp = `Chronic Days` - `No Days`)

g2_pairwise_differences <- g2_composition_all %>%
  select(variable, variable_label, level, outcome_group, pct_weighted, facet_level) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = outcome_group, values_from = pct_weighted) %>%
  mutate(
    non_chronic_minus_no_days = `Non-Chronic Days` - `No Days`,
    chronic_minus_non_chronic = `Chronic Days` - `Non-Chronic Days`
  )

g2_intensity <- predictor_long %>%
  filter(outcome_group == "Chronic Days", !is.na(level)) %>%
  group_by(variable, variable_label, level) %>%
  summarise(
    n_raw = n(),
    mean_chronic_days = weighted_mean(days_absent_chronic, weight_value),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  add_facet_level()

g2_exact_distribution <- predictor_long %>%
  filter(outcome_group == "Chronic Days", !is.na(level)) %>%
  group_by(variable, variable_label, level, days_absent_chronic) %>%
  summarise(weight_sum = sum(weight_value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  tidyr::complete(days_absent_chronic = 1:31, fill = list(weight_sum = 0)) %>%
  mutate(pct_within_level = weight_sum / sum(weight_sum) * 100) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  add_facet_level()

g20 - Composition Across All Three Groups

Graph g20 adds the Non-Chronic Days group to the same canvas. Its grey, brown, and blue group colours match EDA-71 graph g2 so the outcome partition remains visually consistent across reports.

g21 - Composition Difference

g211 - Non-Chronic Minus No Days

g212 - Chronic Minus Non-Chronic Days

g22 - Chronic-Day Intensity

g23 - Exact Positive-Day Distribution

Graph g23 continues the thought of EDA-71’s g23_chronic_vs_other_only_split_distribution: it preserves every exact positive chronic-day value, but replaces the other-only comparison with predisposing-variable levels. Each row has its own 100% conditional denominator.

g24 - Exact Distribution With Composition Tiles

Graph g24 retains g23’s exact-day matrix and adds two composition columns. The respondent-count column uses raw subgroup counts converted to shares, while the weighted-share column uses the pooled survey weight. Each column sums to 100% within a predictor, allowing levels such as age 15-24 to be read in context.

Prepare raw respondent-count shares and weighted shares by predictor level 
g24_composition_tiles <- g2_composition_all %>%
  filter(outcome_group == "Chronic Days") %>%
  select(variable, variable_label, facet_level, pct_raw, pct_weighted) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(pct_raw, pct_weighted),
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "share"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    measure = factor(
      measure,
      levels = c("pct_raw", "pct_weighted"),
      labels = c("Respondent\nshare", "Weighted\nshare")
    )
  )

stopifnot(
  all(
    abs(
      g24_composition_tiles %>%
        group_by(variable, measure) %>%
        summarise(total = sum(share), .groups = "drop") %>%
        pull(total) - 100
    ) < 1e-8
  )
)

Appendix

Student status is excluded because the source field is suppressed and all-missing in the PUMF. The three outcome groups are exhaustive and mutually exclusive; the added graphs now show their complete composition comparison matrix.
